vision: To be the leading institution in Nepal providing world-class UK university education and creating globally competitive professionals.
mission: To provide high-quality, industry-relevant education that empowers students with practical skills, innovative thinking, and ethical values.

programs.name: BSc (Hons) Computer Science
programs.description: A comprehensive program designed to equip students with the technical and theoretical knowledge required for a successful career in the IT industry.

programs.name: BSc (Hons) Cybersecurity
programs.description: A specialized program focused on protecting organizations, networks, and systems from cyber threats and data breaches.

programs.name: BSc (Hons) International Business Management
programs.description: Designed for future business leaders, focusing on international trade, management principles, and global market dynamics.

programs.name: International Master of Business Administration (IMBA)
programs.description: A postgraduate degree aimed at pre-experience applicants to gain a holistic view of modern business management.
modules: Information Management and Business Research Methods

scholarships.name: AAA Scholarship (Attitude, Academics, and Attendance)
scholarships.description: Awarded to the top 10% of students based on their overall performance.
scholarships.benefit: 100% refund of the total college fees paid (excluding admission and university fees).

scholarships.name: International Exposure Scholarship
scholarships.description: Opportunities for top-performing students to gain global experience.
scholarships.benefit: 15-day all-expense-paid summer camp at the University of Wolverhampton, UK, or other partner locations like Thailand.

campus_facilities.labs: State-of-the-art computer labs with high-speed internet and latest software.
campus_facilities.library: A comprehensive resource center with thousands of books, journals, and e-resources accessible from 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM.
campus_facilities.cafeteria: In-campus cafeteria providing healthy and affordable meals and beverages.
campus_facilities.recreation: Facilities for basketball, table tennis, and common areas for student relaxation.
campus_facilities.wifi: Campus-wide free Wi-Fi for all students and staff.
campus_facilities.parking: Dedicated parking space for student scooters and cars.

clubs.name: Herald DevCorps
clubs.focus: Software development, hackathons, and technical workshops.

clubs.name: BIT Club
clubs.focus: Events and networking for IT students.

clubs.name: BIBM Society
clubs.focus: Business events, leadership training, and corporate networking.

clubs.name: Herald Media Club
clubs.focus: Photography, videography, and social media content creation.

clubs.name: Herald Sports Club
clubs.focus: Organizing tournaments and promoting physical fitness.

events.name: Ingenium
events.description: The flagship annual event showcasing student projects, innovation, and creativity.

events.name: Herald DevCamp
events.description: An intensive coding bootcamp and hackathon for aspiring developers.

events.name: Welcome Program
events.description: Orientation and celebration for new incoming batches.

faqs.question: What are the admission requirements at Herald College Kathmandu?
faqs.answer: Applicants must have completed 10+2 or equivalent with a minimum required grade. Specific requirements vary by program.

faqs.question: How can I apply for admission to Herald College Kathmandu?
faqs.answer: You can apply online through our website or visit the admissions office at our campus in Naxal, Kathmandu.

faqs.question: What undergraduate programs are offered at Herald College Kathmandu?
faqs.answer: We offer BSc (Hons) Computer Science, BSc (Hons) Cybersecurity, and BSc (Hons) International Business Management.

faqs.question: What postgraduate programs are available at Herald College Kathmandu?
faqs.answer: We offer the International Master of Business Administration (IMBA).

faqs.question: What is the application deadline for the next intake?
faqs.answer: Deadlines vary by intake (February and September). Please check our website for exact dates.

faqs.question: How much is the application fee?
faqs.answer: The application fee is typically NPR 1,000.

faqs.question: What scholarships are available for students?
faqs.answer: We offer the AAA Scholarship and International Exposure scholarships for top performers.

faqs.question: How can I apply for a scholarship?
faqs.answer: Scholarships are usually merit-based and attendance-based. You are automatically considered if you meet the criteria.

faqs.question: What are the tuition fees for BSc (Hons) Computer Science?
faqs.answer: HCK offers affordable UK education. Please contact the accounts office for the latest fee structure.

faqs.question: What are the tuition fees for International Business Management?
faqs.answer: Fees are structured to be competitive for a UK degree. Contact admissions for a detailed breakdown.

faqs.question: Are there any installment plans for tuition fees?
faqs.answer: Yes, we offer installment plans. Details can be obtained from the accounts department.

faqs.question: What is the duration of the BSc (Hons) Computer Science program?
faqs.answer: It is a 3-year program consisting of 6 semesters.

faqs.question: What is the duration of the International Business Management program?
faqs.answer: It is a 3-year program consisting of 6 semesters.

faqs.question: Is there a cafeteria on campus?
faqs.answer: Yes, a cafeteria is available on campus.

faqs.question: What are the library hours?
faqs.answer: The library is open from 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM on weekdays.

faqs.question: How can I get a student ID card?
faqs.answer: ID cards are issued by the administration office after full enrollment.

faqs.question: What documents are required for admission?
faqs.answer: Academic transcripts, character certificates, citizenship copy, and photos are required.

faqs.question: Can international students apply?
faqs.answer: Yes, international students are welcome to apply.

faqs.question: What is the medium of instruction?
faqs.answer: The medium of instruction is English for all programs.

faqs.question: Are there any entrance exams?
faqs.answer: Some programs may require an interview or a basic assessment test.

faqs.question: How can I prepare for the entrance exam?
faqs.answer: Focus on English proficiency and basic logic/math for the assessment.

faqs.question: What is the contact number for admissions?
faqs.answer: You can reach admissions at +977 9801022637.

faqs.question: What is the email address for inquiries?
faqs.answer: Email us at info@heraldcollege.edu.np.

faqs.question: Where is Herald College Kathmandu located?
faqs.answer: We are located in Naxal, Kathmandu, near Bhatbhateni Supermarket.

faqs.question: How do I reach the campus by public transport?
faqs.answer: Public buses and taxis to Naxal will bring you close to the campus.

faqs.question: Is there parking available on campus?
faqs.answer: Yes, we have parking facilities for student scooters and cars.

faqs.question: What are the office hours?
faqs.answer: 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM, Sunday to Friday.

faqs.question: Who is the principal of Herald College Kathmandu?
faqs.answer: Please visit our leadership page on the website for current details.

faqs.question: What is the college's affiliation?
faqs.answer: We are affiliated with the University of Wolverhampton, UK.

faqs.question: Which university is Herald College Kathmandu affiliated with?
faqs.answer: The University of Wolverhampton, UK.

faqs.question: What is the history of Herald College Kathmandu?
faqs.answer: HCK was established to bring high-quality British education to Nepal at an affordable cost.

faqs.question: What extracurricular activities are available?
faqs.answer: We have various clubs for coding, business, media, and sports.

faqs.question: Are there any student clubs?
faqs.answer: Yes, including Herald DevCorps, BIT Club, and BIBM Society.

faqs.question: How can I join a student club?
faqs.answer: Sign-ups are usually open during the 'Club Fair' at the start of semesters.

faqs.question: What sports facilities are available?
faqs.answer: Basketball, table tennis, and futsal tournaments are organized regularly.

faqs.question: Is there a gym on campus?
faqs.answer: We do not have a dedicated gym, but we promote various sports activities.

faqs.question: Are internships available for students?
faqs.answer: Yes, our career services help students find internships in top IT and business firms.

faqs.question: How does the college support job placement?
faqs.answer: We have an active placement cell and partnerships with leading companies.

faqs.question: What is the alumni network like?
faqs.answer: Our alumni are working in top positions globally and often visit for guest lectures.

faqs.question: Are there any international exchange programs?
faqs.answer: Yes, we offer summer camps at the University of Wolverhampton, UK.

faqs.question: How can I participate in exchange programs?
faqs.answer: Participation is usually merit-based. Keep high grades and attendance.

faqs.question: What is the academic calendar?
faqs.answer: The calendar is shared with students via the portal at the beginning of each semester.

faqs.question: When do classes start?
faqs.answer: Major intakes start in February and September.

faqs.question: What is the grading system?
faqs.answer: We follow the UK grading system (First Class, Upper Second, etc.).

faqs.question: How can I get my transcripts?
faqs.answer: Request them through the administration office or the student portal.

faqs.question: How do I request a recommendation letter?
faqs.answer: Contact your module leader or personal tutor.

faqs.question: What is the dress code?
faqs.answer: Students are expected to dress decently and professionally.

faqs.question: Are laptops required for students?
faqs.answer: Yes, having a laptop is mandatory for practical sessions and assignments.

faqs.question: Is Wi-Fi available on campus?
faqs.answer: Yes, high-speed Wi-Fi is available across the campus.

faqs.question: How do I access online learning resources?
faqs.answer: You can access Canvas and other resources using your university credentials.

faqs.question: What is the process for course registration?
faqs.answer: Registration is done online through the student portal.

faqs.question: How can I change my course?
faqs.answer: Consult the academic counselor within the first two weeks of the semester.

faqs.question: What is the refund policy?
faqs.answer: The policy is detailed in the student handbook. Admission fees are generally non-refundable.

faqs.question: How do I pay my fees?
faqs.answer: Fees can be paid at the accounts office or via online payment gateways like eSewa/Khalti.

faqs.question: Can I pay fees online?
faqs.answer: Yes, online payment options are available through the student portal.

faqs.question: What is the late fee policy?
faqs.answer: Fines are applicable for payments made after the deadline.

faqs.question: How do I apply for financial aid?
faqs.answer: Contact the accounts office for information on available aid and scholarships.

faqs.question: What is the student-to-faculty ratio?
faqs.answer: We maintain a low ratio to ensure personalized attention, typically 15:1.

faqs.question: Who are the faculty members?
faqs.answer: Our faculty consists of industry experts and PhD holders from diverse backgrounds.

faqs.question: How can I contact my professors?
faqs.answer: You can email them or meet them during their specified office hours.

faqs.question: What is the class size?
faqs.answer: Class sizes are kept optimal for interactive learning sessions.

faqs.question: Are there guest lectures?
faqs.answer: Yes, we frequently host international and local experts.

faqs.question: How can I attend seminars and workshops?
faqs.answer: Notifications are sent via email and posted on campus notice boards.

faqs.question: What is the process for transferring credits?
faqs.answer: Submit your transcripts to the academic office for evaluation.

faqs.question: Can I transfer from another college?
faqs.answer: Yes, credit transfers are possible subject to university approval.

faqs.question: What is the process for credit transfer?
faqs.answer: Detailed evaluation of previously completed modules is required.

faqs.question: Are there any part-time courses?
faqs.answer: Currently, we only offer full-time programs.

faqs.question: Can I work while studying?
faqs.answer: While allowed, we recommend focusing on your studies due to the intensive curriculum.

faqs.question: What are the rules for attendance?
faqs.answer: Minimum 80% attendance is required to be eligible for scholarships and exams.

faqs.question: How do I report an absence?
faqs.answer: Inform the administration office or your tutor via email.

faqs.question: What is the process for medical leave?
faqs.answer: Submit a valid medical certificate to the administration office.

faqs.question: How do I get academic counseling?
faqs.answer: Visit the academic support desk or book an appointment with your tutor.

faqs.question: Is there a career counseling service?
faqs.answer: Yes, we have a dedicated career services department.

faqs.question: How do I access mental health support?
faqs.answer: Confidential counseling services are available for students.

faqs.question: What is the emergency contact number?
faqs.answer: Please call +977-1-5970120 for campus emergencies.

faqs.question: How do I report harassment or discrimination?
faqs.answer: We have a zero-tolerance policy. Report immediately to the administration.

faqs.question: What is the anti-ragging policy?
faqs.answer: Ragging is strictly prohibited and can lead to immediate expulsion.

faqs.question: Are there any campus safety measures?
faqs.answer: CCTV surveillance and 24/7 security personnel are present.

faqs.question: How do I get a library card?
faqs.answer: Your student ID card acts as your library card.

faqs.question: What is the borrowing limit for books?
faqs.answer: Usually 3 books for 2 weeks.

faqs.question: How do I renew borrowed books?
faqs.answer: Renew them online through the library portal or at the desk.

faqs.question: What are the penalties for late returns?
faqs.answer: A daily fine is charged for overdue books.

faqs.question: How do I access e-books?
faqs.answer: Login to the University of Wolverhampton's digital library.

faqs.question: What is the process for lost ID cards?
faqs.answer: Report it and pay a small fee for a replacement card.

faqs.question: How do I update my contact information?
faqs.answer: Update it through the 'My Profile' section on the student portal.

faqs.question: How do I get a bonafide certificate?
faqs.answer: Apply at the administration desk with your details.

faqs.question: What is the process for migration certificate?
faqs.answer: Apply after completing your degree through the admin office.

faqs.question: How do I apply for revaluation?
faqs.answer: Applications must be submitted within the deadline after results are out.

faqs.question: What is the process for supplementary exams?
faqs.answer: Details are provided by the exam cell after main results.

faqs.question: How do I get my exam schedule?
faqs.answer: Schedules are posted on the portal and notice boards.

faqs.question: What is the process for exam registration?
faqs.answer: Students are automatically registered once fees are cleared.

faqs.question: How do I get my results?
faqs.answer: Results are published on the official student portal.

faqs.question: What is the process for convocation?
faqs.answer: Convocation is held annually for graduates; registration opens months before.

faqs.question: How do I get my degree certificate?
faqs.answer: Certificates are issued by the University of Wolverhampton.

faqs.question: What is the process for duplicate certificates?
faqs.answer: A formal application and fee are required for duplicates.

faqs.question: How do I apply for a transfer certificate?
faqs.answer: Submit a request to the administration office.

faqs.question: What is the process for character certificate?
faqs.answer: Issued upon completion of the course from the admin office.

faqs.question: How do I get a no objection certificate?
faqs.answer: Request it for specific purposes from the administration.

faqs.question: What is the grievance redressal process?
faqs.answer: Use the online grievance form or visit the student affairs head.

faqs.question: How do I submit feedback?
faqs.answer: Participate in mid-semester and end-semester feedback surveys.

faqs.question: What is the process for student elections?
faqs.answer: Elections for club leaders and council are held every academic year.

faqs.question: How do I participate in cultural events?
faqs.answer: Join the relevant clubs and watch for event sign-ups.

faqs.question: What is the annual fest?
faqs.answer: Ingenium is our biggest annual celebration.

faqs.question: How do I register for events?
faqs.answer: Links are usually provided in official student emails.

faqs.question: Are there any workshops for skill development?
faqs.answer: Yes, workshops on Soft Skills, Coding, and Entrepreneurship are common.

faqs.question: How do I join the student council?
faqs.answer: Apply during the nomination period announced by the college.

faqs.question: Do you provide hostel facilities?
faqs.answer: We do not have hostels but can help you find one nearby.

faqs.question: What transport options are available for students?
faqs.answer: Public transport is highly accessible; limited on-campus parking exists.

faqs.question: What is the placement rate?
faqs.answer: Our graduates have an excellent track record with over 85% placement.

faqs.question: What are the qualifications of the faculty?
faqs.answer: Our faculty includes experienced academicians and industry veterans.

faqs.question: Is there a student counseling service?
faqs.answer: Yes, both academic and personal counseling are available.

faqs.question: Are there any student exchange programs?
faqs.answer: Yes, primarily with the University of Wolverhampton, UK.

faqs.question: How do I join a student club?
faqs.answer: Visit the club desks during the orientation week.

faqs.question: How do I access online learning resources?
faqs.answer: Use the student portal login details provided during induction.

faqs.question: What is the process for credit transfer?
faqs.answer: Submit official transcripts and module descriptors for review.

faqs.question: Is Herald College Kathmandu expensive?
faqs.answer: We focus on providing affordable UK education in Nepal.

faqs.question: Does Herald College provide jobs?
faqs.answer: We provide placement support and networking opportunities to help you get hired.

faqs.question: Is the degree from UK recognized in Nepal?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is fully recognized and has equivalence from TU.

faqs.question: Can I study BSc CS if I am from a Biology background?
faqs.answer: Yes, as long as you meet the minimum grade requirements in 10+2.

faqs.question: What is special about Herald College?
faqs.answer: Direct affiliation with a top UK university and a focus on practical skills.

faqs.question: Do I need IELTS for admission?
faqs.answer: English proficiency is required; check with admissions for specific test requirements.

faqs.question: What is L-GEMS?
faqs.answer: It is a special scholarship and support program for deserving students.

faqs.question: How many days a week is the college open?
faqs.answer: Classes are usually held 5 days a week, Sunday to Friday.

faqs.question: Are there any hidden costs?
faqs.answer: No, all major fees are disclosed during the admission process.

faqs.question: What happens if I fail a module?
faqs.answer: You will have an opportunity to resit the exam or retake the module.

faqs.question: Is there any support for starting a business?
faqs.answer: Yes, the BIBM society and entrepreneurship workshops provide guidance.

faqs.question: What are the laptop requirements?
faqs.answer: A minimum of i5 processor, 8GB RAM, and 256GB SSD is recommended for IT students.

faqs.question: Can I use the labs after class hours?
faqs.answer: Yes, labs are open until 6:00 PM for student use.

faqs.question: Is there a prayer room on campus?
faqs.answer: Please ask the administration for designated quiet spaces.

faqs.question: Are there any international students currently?
faqs.answer: Yes, we have a diverse student body from various backgrounds.

faqs.question: What is the average salary after graduation?
faqs.answer: It varies by industry, but IT graduates often start at attractive packages.

faqs.question: How do I become a student ambassador?
faqs.answer: Apply during the recruitment call from the marketing department.

faqs.question: Is there any financial support for student projects?
faqs.answer: Yes, standout projects can receive funding and mentorship.

faqs.question: What is the policy on smoking and alcohol?
faqs.answer: Herald College is a strictly smoke-free and alcohol-free zone.

faqs.question: Are there lockers available for students?
faqs.answer: Limited locker facilities are available; contact administration.

faqs.question: How do I get my graduation photos?
faqs.answer: These are usually shared via the official alumni portal or email link.

faqs.question: Is there a bus facility?
faqs.answer: The college does not operate its own bus service currently.

faqs.question: How do I prepare for the Internship Fair?
faqs.answer: Update your LinkedIn profile and attend the workshop on 'How to land an internship'.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Startup Weekend'?
faqs.answer: A 54-hour event where you build a startup from scratch.

faqs.question: How do I get a recommendation for a higher study abroad?
faqs.answer: Consistently perform well and engage in research/projects.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Leadership Program'?
faqs.answer: A series of workshops designed to build future leaders.

faqs.question: How do I join the Leadership Program?
faqs.answer: It is often open to club leaders and class representatives.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Social'?
faqs.answer: An informal networking event for students to relax and connect.

faqs.question: How do I get my portal password reset?
faqs.answer: Visit the IT support office on the ground floor.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Green' initiative?
faqs.answer: Our efforts to make the campus more eco-friendly and sustainable.

faqs.question: How do I join the Green Initiative?
faqs.answer: Sign up for the environmental awareness campaigns organized by the council.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Sports Week'?
faqs.answer: A week dedicated to various indoor and outdoor sports competitions.

faqs.question: How do I get a certificate of completion?
faqs.answer: It is issued along with your final transcript after the course ends.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Art Gallery'?
faqs.answer: A space where student artwork and photography are displayed during events.

faqs.question: How do I display my work in the Art Gallery?
faqs.answer: Submit your work to the Media or Cultural club for selection.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Music Fest'?
faqs.answer: An event featuring student bands and musical performances.

faqs.question: How do I join the college band?
faqs.answer: Auditions are held at the beginning of the year by the Cultural Club.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Quiz'?
faqs.answer: An annual inter-batch quiz competition with exciting prizes.

faqs.question: How do I register for the Herald Quiz?
faqs.answer: Form a team of three and register at the BIT Club desk.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Debate'?
faqs.answer: A competition focusing on critical thinking and public speaking skills.

faqs.question: How do I join the debating society?
faqs.answer: Join the BIBM society as they handle most debate-related events.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Coding Challenge'?
faqs.answer: A weekly online coding contest for all IT students.

faqs.question: How do I participate in the Coding Challenge?
faqs.answer: Check the DevCorps Slack channel for weekly links.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Business Case'?
faqs.answer: A competition where you solve real-world business problems.

faqs.question: How do I register for the Business Case?
faqs.answer: Register via the BIBM society portal.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Film Festival'?
faqs.answer: A showcase of short films and documentaries made by students.

faqs.question: How do I submit my film?
faqs.answer: Submit a digital copy to the Media Club before the deadline.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Book Club'?
faqs.answer: A group for students who love reading and discussing literature.

faqs.question: How do I join the Book Club?
faqs.answer: Sign up at the library notice board.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Gaming'?
faqs.answer: E-sports tournaments organized by the BIT Club.

faqs.question: How do I register for E-sports?
faqs.answer: Watch for the registration links on the BIT Club social media pages.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Volunteer'?
faqs.answer: A program where you can help in organizing college events.

faqs.question: How do I become a volunteer?
faqs.answer: Apply when there is a 'Call for Volunteers' for any event.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Internship Fair'?
faqs.answer: An event specifically for students looking for summer internships.

faqs.question: What do I learn in Programming Fundamentals?
faqs.answer: You learn basic logic, control structures, and your first programming language (usually Python or Java).

faqs.question: What is the Web Development module about?
faqs.answer: It covers HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and backend frameworks to build full-stack web applications.

faqs.question: Is Database module hard?
faqs.answer: It is logical and focuses on SQL and database design principles; regular practice makes it easy.

faqs.question: What is Software Engineering focus?
faqs.answer: It focuses on the entire lifecycle of software development, from requirement gathering to testing.

faqs.question: What is Algorithms and Data Structures?
faqs.answer: It is a core module that teaches you how to write efficient code and manage data effectively.

faqs.question: Will I learn AI in BSc CS?
faqs.answer: Yes, we have a dedicated module on Artificial Intelligence covering machine learning and logic.

faqs.question: What is Mobile App Development module?
faqs.answer: It teaches you how to build apps for Android and iOS platforms.

faqs.question: Do I learn Cloud Computing?
faqs.answer: Yes, you learn about virtualization, AWS, and cloud-native application development.

faqs.question: What is Object Oriented Programming (OOP)?
faqs.answer: A programming paradigm that uses 'objects' to design applications; it is a fundamental module.

faqs.question: What is Data Science and Analytics module?
faqs.answer: It covers data mining, visualization, and statistical methods to extract insights from data.

faqs.question: What is Network Security module?
faqs.answer: A cybersecurity module focusing on protecting data transmission and network protocols.

faqs.question: What is Risk Management in Cybersecurity?
faqs.answer: Learning how to identify, assess, and mitigate security risks in an organization.

faqs.question: What is Cyber Threat Intelligence?
faqs.answer: Collecting and analyzing information about potential cyber attacks.

faqs.question: What is Digital Forensics?
faqs.answer: Techniques for recovering and investigating material found in digital devices, often for legal evidence.

faqs.question: What is Ethical Hacking module?
faqs.answer: Authorized hacking to find and fix security vulnerabilities.

faqs.question: What is Business Management Fundamentals?
faqs.answer: Introduction to organizational structure, leadership, and management principles.

faqs.question: What is International Marketing?
faqs.answer: Marketing strategies tailored for different global cultures and regulations.

faqs.question: What is Human Resource Management (HRM)?
faqs.answer: Focuses on recruitment, training, and managing employees effectively.

faqs.question: What is Financial Accounting module?
faqs.answer: Learning how to record, summarize, and report financial transactions of a business.

faqs.question: What is Strategic Management?
faqs.answer: Analyzing the long-term goals and competitive positioning of a company.

faqs.question: What is Entrepreneurship module?
faqs.answer: Skills and knowledge required to start and manage your own business venture.

faqs.question: What is Economics for Business?
faqs.answer: How micro and macro-economic factors affect business decisions.

faqs.question: What is Consumer Behavior module?
faqs.answer: Studying why and how people buy products and services.

faqs.question: What is Supply Chain Management?
faqs.answer: Managing the flow of goods and services from production to the final consumer.

faqs.question: What is Masters Learning module?
faqs.answer: An IMBA module that prepares you for advanced postgraduate research and study skills.

faqs.question: What is Managing Financial Performance?
faqs.answer: Advanced financial analysis and decision-making for managers.

faqs.question: What is Creativity and Innovation module?
faqs.answer: Developing out-of-the-box thinking for business problem solving.

faqs.question: What is Strategic Business Planning?
faqs.answer: Crafting comprehensive business plans for global growth.

faqs.question: What is Operations and Project Management?
faqs.answer: Techniques for managing complex projects and optimizing business operations.

faqs.question: What is Information Management module?
faqs.answer: How organizations use technology and information systems to gain an edge.

faqs.question: What is Business Research Methods?
faqs.answer: Learning quantitative and qualitative methods for business data analysis.

faqs.question: How to handle a Canvas login error?
faqs.answer: Clear your browser cache, try a different browser, or contact the IT support desk if the issue persists.

faqs.question: Where is the library clearance form available?
faqs.answer: You can download it from the student portal or collect it from the library front desk.

faqs.question: How to apply for a semester break?
faqs.answer: Apply through the academic office with a valid reason before the semester ends.

faqs.question: Where to submit the scholarship application?
faqs.answer: Submit it to the accounts or admissions office by the announced deadline.

faqs.question: How to join the student council?
faqs.answer: Participate in the annual elections or apply for nominated positions when announced.

faqs.question: What is the procedure for module resit?
faqs.answer: Register for the resit exam at the exam cell after paying the required fee.

faqs.question: How to get a duplicate transcript?
faqs.answer: Apply at the admin desk, pay the duplicate fee, and provide a valid reason.

faqs.question: Where is the student counseling office located?
faqs.answer: It is on the first floor, providing a quiet and confidential environment.

faqs.question: How to report a lost student ID?
faqs.answer: Inform the administration immediately to block it and apply for a new one.

faqs.question: How to book a lab for group projects?
faqs.answer: Check for availability at the lab assistant's desk and book a slot.

faqs.question: What are the common IT partners of HCK?
faqs.answer: We have strong links with companies like Leapfrog, Deerhold, Logpoint, and F1Soft.

faqs.question: Does HCK have an entrepreneurship cell?
faqs.answer: Yes, we have an incubation center that supports student startups.

faqs.question: How to get my university degree equivalence in Nepal?
faqs.answer: Submit your degree and transcripts to the Curriculum Development Centre (CDC) of TU; the college provides supporting letters.

faqs.question: Is there a student grievance portal?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can submit grievances anonymously through the link on the student portal.

faqs.question: How to volunteer for the Blood Donation Camp?
faqs.answer: Register with the Social Service Club representatives on the day of the event.

faqs.question: What are the canteen hours?
faqs.answer: The canteen is open from 7:30 AM to 5:30 PM.

faqs.question: How to get a letter for a passport application?
faqs.answer: Request a 'Bonafide Certificate for Passport' from the administration office.

faqs.question: Does HCK offer placement in the UK?
faqs.answer: While the degree is from the UK, placements are primarily with leading firms in Nepal and international remote roles.

faqs.question: How to register for the 'Ingenium' showcase?
faqs.answer: Submit your project abstract to the technical committee when the call is released.

faqs.question: Are there any student exchange programs with Thailand?
faqs.answer: Yes, we often have exposure trips to business hubs in Thailand for IBM students.

faqs.question: How to use the library e-resources at home?
faqs.answer: Log in to the University of Wolverhampton portal using your student credentials.

faqs.question: What to do if I miss a midterm exam?
faqs.answer: Contact the academic office immediately with a valid medical certificate or proof of emergency.

faqs.question: How to find a personal tutor's office?
faqs.answer: Check the tutor directory on the portal or ask at the reception desk.

faqs.question: Can I use the common room during lectures?
faqs.answer: No, the common room should only be used during breaks to maintain academic decorum.

faqs.question: How to get a recommendation for a bank loan?
faqs.answer: Request a 'Fee Estimate Letter' and 'Bonafide Certificate' from the accounts office.

faqs.question: Is there a designated area for group study?
faqs.answer: Yes, the library has specific 'Collaboration Zones' for group work.

faqs.question: How to join the Herald Media Club?
faqs.answer: Fill out the membership form during the club recruitment week.

faqs.question: What is the 'L-GEMS' program?
faqs.answer: It is an exclusive mentorship and support program for high-potential students.

faqs.question: How to apply for the 'Employability Award'?
faqs.answer: Record your professional development activities on the portal and submit for review in the final year.

faqs.question: Does HCK provide training for IELTS?
faqs.answer: We provide guidance and resources, but students often take external tests as required.

faqs.question: How to get a duplicate ID card?
faqs.answer: Pay the replacement fee at the accounts office and submit the receipt to administration.

faqs.question: What is the policy on laptops in the library?
faqs.answer: Laptops are allowed and encouraged; please use headphones if listening to audio.

faqs.question: Where to find the latest academic calendar?
faqs.answer: It is pinned on the student portal home page and campus notice boards.

faqs.question: How to handle a disagreement with a tutor?
faqs.answer: Try to resolve it directly first; if not, speak to your Personal Tutor or the Academic Head.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Excellence' award given for?
faqs.answer: It is awarded for outstanding contribution to college life, clubs, and overall leadership.

faqs.question: How to access old exam papers?
faqs.answer: The library and student portal have archives of previous years' papers for practice.

faqs.question: Does HCK have a sports day?
faqs.answer: Yes, we have a week-long 'Sports Meet' annually with various competitions.

faqs.question: How to join the BIT Club committee?
faqs.answer: Apply for executive roles during the annual committee reshuffle announced on social media.

faqs.question: What is the BIBM Networking event?
faqs.answer: A semi-formal event where IBM students interact with leaders from the corporate world.

faqs.question: How to get a certificate for 'Herald DevCamp'?
faqs.answer: Certificates are distributed at the end of the camp to all successful participants.

faqs.question: Can I bring my own food to the canteen?
faqs.answer: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own meals and use the canteen seating area.

faqs.question: How to report a broken link on the portal?
faqs.answer: Email the IT support team with a screenshot of the issue.

faqs.question: What is the 'Soft Skills' workshop about?
faqs.answer: It covers presentation skills, emotional intelligence, and professional ethics.

faqs.question: How to apply for an international master's after HCK?
faqs.answer: Your UK degree makes you eligible for masters worldwide; our counselors can help with the application.

faqs.question: Is there a designated area for meditation?
faqs.answer: Ask the student affairs office for access to quiet spaces on the campus.

faqs.question: How to join the social service wing?
faqs.answer: Register with the social service coordinators during the orientation fair.

faqs.question: What to do if I lose my belongings on campus?
faqs.answer: Check the 'Lost and Found' box at the main security desk.

faqs.question: How to update my emergency contact number?
faqs.answer: Log in to the student portal and edit your profile details.

faqs.question: Are there any student led workshops?
faqs.answer: Yes, senior students often conduct workshops on specialized tech through DevCorps.

faqs.question: How to suggest a new club?
faqs.answer: Submit a formal proposal with at least 10 interested students to the Student Union.

faqs.question: What is the policy on campus decorations?
faqs.answer: Clubs must get approval from the administration before putting up posters or decorations.

faqs.question: How to get a letter for a driving license?
faqs.answer: Request a 'Bonafide Certificate' stating your current address and status.

faqs.question: Does HCK have a music room?
faqs.answer: Currently, we have equipment that the Cultural Club uses in designated spaces.

faqs.question: How to join the 'Code-Off' challenges?
faqs.answer: Join the DevCorps Slack or Discord server for real-time notifications.

faqs.question: What is the 'Business Case' competition about?
faqs.answer: You are given a real business problem and have a limited time to propose a solution.

faqs.question: How to get involved in the Film Festival?
faqs.answer: Submit your short film to the Media Club or volunteer as a crew member.

faqs.question: Is there a photography competition?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Media Club organizes 'Herald Clicks' annually.

faqs.question: How to access the campus after 6 PM?
faqs.answer: Students generally need special permission for late-night project work.

faqs.question: Where is the drinking water dispenser?
faqs.answer: Dispensers are located near the elevator and stairs on every floor.

faqs.question: How to report an incident on campus?
faqs.answer: Speak to any staff member or use the 'Report Incident' feature on the portal.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Green' club?
faqs.answer: A club dedicated to environmental sustainability and campus greening.

faqs.question: How to get a scholarship for the final year?
faqs.answer: Keep your grades high and maintain 80% attendance in all previous years.

faqs.question: Are there any research labs?
faqs.answer: We have specialized labs for data science and cybersecurity research.

faqs.question: How to get my project showcased in Ingenium?
faqs.answer: Apply when the portal opens for Ingenium project submissions.

faqs.question: What to do if I feel overwhelmed with studies?
faqs.answer: Immediately contact the student counselor or your personal tutor for support.

faqs.question: How to get a certificate for being a class rep?
faqs.answer: Request it from the administration at the end of the academic year.

faqs.question: Does HCK have an annual magazine?
faqs.answer: Yes, 'The Herald Voice' is published annually by the media team.

faqs.question: How to contribute a poem or story to the magazine?
faqs.answer: Email your contribution to the editor at media@heraldcollege.edu.np.

faqs.question: What is the best way to travel to HCK?
faqs.answer: Public buses to Naxal or ride-sharing apps like Pathao/InDrive are very convenient.

faqs.question: How to find the accounts office?
faqs.answer: It is located on the ground floor, near the main entrance.

faqs.question: Where to submit the library books during holidays?
faqs.answer: There is a drop-box near the library entrance for holiday returns.

faqs.question: How to get a recommendation for a summer job?
faqs.answer: Visit the career services office with your updated CV and transcript.

faqs.question: What is the policy on eating inside labs?
faqs.answer: Eating and drinking (except water) is strictly prohibited in all computer labs.

faqs.question: How to join the Herald Gaming community?
faqs.answer: Connect with the BIT Club gaming coordinators for the latest tournament links.

faqs.question: What to do if I forget my student ID?
faqs.answer: Enter your details in the visitor log at the gate and visit admin for a temporary pass.

faqs.question: How to get my degree recognized for a government job?
faqs.answer: Obtain the TU equivalence certificate using the documents provided by HCK.

faqs.question: What is the focus of the 'Data Science' research group?
faqs.answer: They focus on predictive analytics and big data processing in the local context.

faqs.question: How to apply for the 'Innovation Hub' grant?
faqs.answer: Submit a detailed project proposal and budget during the annual grant call.

faqs.question: Is there any support for mental wellbeing?
faqs.answer: Yes, we have professional counselors available for all students.

faqs.question: How to become a 'Herald Ambassador'?
faqs.answer: Apply when the marketing department opens positions for student representatives.

faqs.question: What to do if I witness bullying?
faqs.answer: Report it immediately to the student affairs head or any faculty member.

faqs.question: How to access the 'Herald Alumni' portal?
faqs.answer: You will receive an invitation link on your personal email after graduation.

faqs.question: Is there a first-aid room?
faqs.answer: Yes, the administration floor has a dedicated first-aid area.

faqs.question: How to get involved in the 'Green Initiative'?
faqs.answer: Join the eco-friendly drives and workshops organized by the Green Club.

faqs.question: What to do if I have a grievance against a staff member?
faqs.answer: Follow the formal grievance procedure outlined in the student handbook.

faqs.question: How to get a letter for an educational tour?
faqs.answer: Request a 'Tour Recommendation' from the administration office with the tour details.

faqs.question: Are there any student led businesses on campus?
faqs.answer: Yes, several student startups operate from our incubation center.

faqs.question: How to join the 'Herald Coding Challenge' committee?
faqs.answer: Executive roles are open to active DevCorps members every semester.

faqs.question: What is the best way to stay updated with college news?
faqs.answer: Follow our official social media pages and check your student email daily.

faqs.question: How to get my final transcripts after graduation?
faqs.answer: They are sent to the college by the University of Wolverhampton and you can collect them after clearing all dues.

faqs.question: Where is the student lounge?
faqs.answer: It is a large, comfortable area on the second floor for students to relax and socialise.

faqs.question: How to get a certificate for community service?
faqs.answer: The Social Service Club provides certificates for active volunteers at the end of the year.

faqs.question: What to do if I want to restart my course?
faqs.answer: Consult the academic head and admissions office for the restart process and fee implications.

faqs.question: How to get a letter for a foreign visa application?
faqs.answer: Request a 'Bonafide Certificate for Visa' from the admin office.

faqs.question: Does HCK have an annual day celebration?
faqs.answer: Yes, 'Ingenium' also serves as our major annual celebration day.

faqs.question: How to join the 'Business Case' study group?
faqs.answer: Connect with the BIBM society for their weekly case study sessions.

faqs.question: What to do if I have questions about my tuition fees?
faqs.answer: Visit the accounts office on the ground floor for any financial inquiries.

faqs.question: How to get my project published in a journal?
faqs.answer: Work closely with your supervisor and the research committee for guidance.

faqs.question: Is there a dedicated space for silence?
faqs.answer: The upper floors of the library are designated 'Quiet Zones' for silent study.

faqs.question: How to apply for the 'Herald Leadership' award?
faqs.answer: Nominations are usually opened at the end of the academic year.

faqs.question: What is the 'Herald Tech Fest' week?
faqs.answer: A week full of tech talks, workshops, and inter-college competitions.

faqs.question: How to contribute to the college social media?
faqs.answer: Connect with the Media Club or the marketing department if you have great content.

faqs.question: What to do if I lose my locker key?
faqs.answer: Report it to the administration and pay for a lock replacement.

faqs.question: How to join the 'Herald Startup Weekend' team?
faqs.answer: Register during the event announcement period; you can join as a solo or with a team.

faqs.question: What to do if I want to suggest an improvement for the campus?
faqs.answer: Use the 'Suggestion Box' near the reception or email the Student Union.

faqs.question: How to get a certificate for the 'Herald Sports' event?
faqs.answer: Certificates are distributed during the closing ceremony of the sports week.

faqs.question: What to do if I have a medical emergency during a lecture?
faqs.answer: Inform your tutor immediately; they will coordinate with security for first aid or hospital transport.

faqs.question: How to join the 'Herald Alumni' networking events?
faqs.answer: Keep your contact info updated to receive invitations to alumni mixers and talks.

faqs.question: Where to find the student handbook?
faqs.answer: A digital copy is available on the student portal under 'Important Documents'.

faqs.question: Where is the exam timetable?
faqs.answer: For the latest exam timetable, please contact the Registry Team (RT) directly or check the 'Academic' section on your student portal.

faqs.question: Is the campus safe?
faqs.answer: Yes, Herald College Kathmandu is highly safe with 24/7 security personnel, comprehensive CCTV surveillance, and strict entry policies to ensure a secure environment for all students.
